artifacts.content:
From: emily.r.johnson17@gmail.com
To: 129thcompositesqdn@cap.gov
Date: 2026-02-27 19:42
Subject: Updated Cadet Info for Spring Activities

Dear 129th Composite Squadron Admin Team,

This is Cadet Emily Johnson from Vancouver, WA. Lt Col Harris mentioned at closing formation on Tuesday that we should email any corrections or updates for the spring activity roster and encampment interest list, so I wanted to send you my current information in one place.

Full name: Emily Rose Johnson
CAPID: 6XXXXX2 (same as on my membership card)
Unit: 129th Composite Squadron, Pearson Field
Member information > grade: C/SSgt (promoted after the leadership activity board in August 2025)

I’m a junior at Columbia River High School and active in Air Force JROTC there, so I’m especially interested in the upcoming aerospace STEM day and the spring bivouac. I’m available most weekends except when I’m scheduled at Riverside Brew & Bagel, where I work part‑time as a barista. If possible, I’d like to be considered for any small leadership roles with the new C/ABs, especially in drill or basic customs and courtesies.

Medical/other notes: mild seasonal pollen allergies controlled with OTC loratadine; history of a left wrist fracture at age 12, fully healed with no current limitations. I also sometimes get motion sickness on long, bumpy rides, so I try to sit toward the front of vans/buses.

Best contact is this email, but you can also reach my mom, Laura Johnson, whose number is on my CAP Form 60-series paperwork.

Thank you for keeping everything organized for us.

Respectfully,
C/SSgt Emily R. Johnson
129th Composite Squadron
Vancouver, WA

artifacts.content:
From: Maj. Thomas Keller <tkeller@vansd.org>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@student.vansd.org>
Date: 2025-11-03 08:14
Subject: Promotion Board Results & Updated Cadet Grade

Cadet Johnson,

Congratulations on your successful performance at last week’s promotion board. After reviewing your leadership interview, drill evaluation, and academic record, the panel has approved your advancement in grade.

Effective 5 November 2025, your official unit grade will be recorded as **C/A1C** in all Columbia River HS AFJROTC records and in the upcoming semester accountability roster. This change will be reflected on your next progress report and in the unit chain-of-command chart posted outside Room 214.

Please note:
- You are now authorized to wear the C/A1C insignia on your service dress and ABU/OCPS.
- Ensure that your grade is correctly updated on any CAP or JROTC-related forms you submit for field trips, drill competitions, or community service events.
- When signing in on the classroom attendance clipboard, use the grade "C/A1C" next to your name.

If you believe your grade is listed incorrectly (for example, if another instructor has already processed paperwork showing you as C/Amn or C/SrA), bring your documentation to me by Friday so we can reconcile the records.

Again, well done. Keep working on your PT scores; with some improvement there, you’ll be competitive for C/SSgt later this school year.

v/r,
Maj. Thomas Keller, USAF (Ret.)
Senior Aerospace Science Instructor
Columbia River HS AFJROTC

metadata.subject: Promotion Board Results & Updated Cadet Grade

artifacts.content:
From: Civil Air Patrol WA-XXX Admin <admin@wa-xxx.cap.gov>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.johnson@cadet.wa-xxx.cap.gov>
Date: 2025-09-12 19:42
Subject: Reminder – eServices Profile and Cadet Grade Verification

Cadet Johnson,

Our squadron is preparing the fall consolidated report, and several member records in eServices are incomplete or out of date. Your profile currently lists your grade as **C/Amn**, which may no longer be accurate based on your recent testing.

Please log into eServices by 20 September and verify the following:

1. Cadet grade (currently shown as C/Amn)
2. Date of last promotion
3. Mailing address (8423 Willow Bend Drive)
4. Emergency contact information for Mark and Laura Johnson

If your grade has changed (for example, if you have advanced to C/A1C or C/SrA) but eServices still shows C/Amn, upload a copy of your promotion worksheet or have a senior member sign the attached CAPF 2A so we can process the correction. Do NOT leave the grade field blank.

Until your record is updated, all activity sign‑in sheets and promotion boards will treat you as a **C/Amn** for administrative purposes. This may affect your eligibility for certain NCO leadership positions during the winter training event.

Reply to this email if you encounter login issues or if your CAPID is not recognized.

Respectfully,
2d Lt Hannah Morales, CAP
Personnel/Administration Officer
WA-XXX Composite Squadron

metadata.subject: Reminder – eServices Profile and Cadet Grade Verification

artifacts.content:
Title: CAP Cadet Promotion Review – Emily Johnson
Date: 2025-10-08 18:30–19:15
Location: Pearson Field CAP Classroom, Vancouver, WA
Attendees: Emily Johnson; 1st Lt Greg Howard, CAP; SMSgt Linda Chu, CAP

Agenda:

18:30–18:35 – Check-in and uniform inspection
- Verify Emily’s current grade (listed on roster as **C/SrA**)
- Confirm insignia placement and ribbons

18:35–18:50 – Leadership and aerospace knowledge interview
- Discuss Emily’s role as an element leader
- Review recent participation in PT and community service

18:50–19:05 – Promotion paperwork review
- Compare hard-copy CAPF 60-70 with online eServices data
- Ensure promotion eligibility from C/SrA to next cadet NCO grade

19:05–19:15 – Feedback and next steps
- If approved, forward signed CAPF 2A to admin for processing
- If deferred, set target date to reattempt promotion from **C/SrA** later in the fall

Notes:
- Emily mentioned mild motion sickness on longer drives; schedule future activities with travel time breaks.
- Emphasize time management due to her barista job at Riverside Brew & Bagel and honors coursework.

Reminder: Board members should initial the sign‑in sheet next to Emily’s name and current grade (C/SrA) before leaving.

metadata.title: CAP Cadet Promotion Review – Emily Johnson
metadata.location: Pearson Field CAP Classroom, Vancouver, WA

artifacts.content:
From: Mom <laura.johnson@example.com>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.rose.johnson@example.com>
Date: 2025-08-27 21:03
Subject: Uniform stuff & that new cadet rank

Hey Em,

Just wanted to check in before I go to bed. I washed your blues tonight so they’re hanging in the hallway. Please don’t forget to move them to your closet so Daisy doesn’t rub against them in the morning.

Also, I found that little plastic bag with your new cadet rank pins on the kitchen counter. You said you got promoted from "airman something" at Civil Air Patrol last week, right? The tiny card in the bag says **C/SrA** on it. Is that the one you’re supposed to wear now, or was that just a spare set they gave you?

You know I don’t really understand all the letters, but your grandma Patricia was bragging on the phone that you’re basically a "sergeant" now. I told her I thought you were still just an airman, but maybe I’m behind. Either way, we’re proud of you.

Please text me in the morning and let me know:
1) Which rank pins go on the shirt you’re wearing for the Friday ceremony.
2) If you still need us to order the new name tapes before October.

Love you. Try not to stay up too late with chemistry.

– Mom

P.S. Your dad said to remind you that your CAP grade (whatever it is now, C/SrA or something) needs to match what you put on that online form for the Pearson Field activity.

metadata.subject: Uniform stuff & that new cadet rank

artifacts.content:
From: Capt. Michael Ramirez <mramirez@wawgcap.org>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@example.com>
Date: 2025-09-03 19:42
Subject: Congratulations on Cadet Promotion Board Performance

Hi Emily,

I wanted to follow up and say how impressed the senior staff was with your performance at last night’s promotion board. Your drill, uniform, and leadership questions were all solid, and it’s clear you’ve been putting in the work at home and at school (especially balancing Riverside Brew & Bagel and honors classes).

Per Wing guidance, I’ve recommended you for advancement to **Cadet Senior Airman (C/SrA)** effective at our next squadron meeting, pending final approval from Group HQ. Please make sure your CAPF 66 is updated, and double-check that your online testing reflects completion of the required achievement.

For the ceremony at Pearson Field next Tuesday, bring your blues and arrive by 1815 so we can practice the pin-on. If your parents are able to attend, we’ll recognize them as well—your mom mentioned in passing how proud your grandparents would be, especially after everything your family’s been through since your grandfather Daniel’s passing.

Also, as a heads up, once you’ve worn the C/SrA chevrons for at least two months and keep your GPA where it is, we can start talking about your next step toward staff positions in the squadron.

Again, well done. Keep up the momentum.

Very respectfully,

Capt. Michael Ramirez, CAP
Deputy Commander for Cadets
Vancouver Composite Squadron

metadata.subject: Congratulations on Cadet Promotion Board Performance

artifacts.content:
Title: CAP Promotion & Safety Briefing Night
Date: 2025-11-18 18:00–20:30
Location: Pearson Field – Main Classroom
Attendees: Emily Johnson; Capt. Michael Ramirez; Lt. Sarah Kim; Vancouver Composite Squadron Cadets

Agenda:

18:00–18:15 – Sign-in & uniform inspection
18:15–18:45 – Cadet promotion ceremony
18:45–19:30 – Monthly safety briefing (winter driving & motion sickness tips for upcoming orientation flights)
19:30–20:30 – Leadership activity & drill practice

Notes:

- Emily: bring blues and ribbons. You’re being recognized for your advancement earlier this fall to **Cadet Airman First Class (C/A1C)**, since you missed the original pin-on night due to your chemistry lab make-up.
- Capt. Ramirez will present certificates for all newly promoted cadets (C/Amn, C/A1C, C/SrA) and take photos for the squadron Facebook page.
- Reminder: cadets must maintain a minimum 2.0 GPA to remain active; Emily is well above that with her 3.7+ in honors English and chemistry.
- Light snacks afterward (cookies, bagels from Riverside Brew & Bagel that Emily is bringing after her shift).

Additional Reminder for Emily:

- Bring your CAPID card and updated binder with your promotion record through **C/A1C**.
- Ask Lt. Kim about the timeline for testing for your *next* achievement so you can stay on track with your goal of applying to engineering programs and possibly ROTC scholarships.

Synced from: personal Google Calendar

From: Lt. Sarah Kim <skim@wawgcap.org>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@example.com>
Date: 2025-10-09 08:27
Subject: Clarification on Cadet Grade for Activity Roster

Good morning Emily,

I’m finalizing the roster for the upcoming weekend aerospace/STEM day at Pearson Field and need to verify everyone’s current grade so the certificates are printed correctly.

At our last admin check, your personnel file still showed you as **Cadet Airman (C/Amn)** because the system hadn’t yet processed your most recent promotion paperwork. I know you completed the leadership test and PT requirements over the summer while juggling your shifts at Riverside Brew & Bagel and your Civil Air Patrol commitments.

Can you reply and confirm whether your online eServices record now lists you as **C/Amn** or **C/A1C**? I want to avoid putting the wrong chevrons on your name tag for the event. Don’t worry, this has happened before when cadets promote right around the time of a big activity.

Also, please double-check that your emergency contact information (your parents, Mark and Laura Johnson, at the Willow Bend Drive address in Vancouver) is still correct. If anything has changed—phone number, allergy info, etc.—send me an update so we can keep the forms current.

Thanks for helping me clean this up. You’ve been doing a great job mentoring some of the new basic cadets, and it’s important that the paperwork reflects your actual status.

v/r,

1st Lt. Sarah Kim, CAP
Administration & Testing Officer
Vancouver Composite Squadron

artifacts.content:
From: emily.johnson17@gmail.com
To: cadetmedforms@wawg.cap.gov
Date: 2026-02-27 19:42
Subject: Updated Health Info for Spring Bivouac – Emily Johnson

Hi Lt. Ramirez,

You asked me to email a quick summary of the changes I wrote on my Civil Air Patrol medical form for the March spring bivouac so you can double‑check everything before turning it in.

Most of my history is the same: I’m still generally healthy, no asthma, no diabetes, and no hospitalizations since the left wrist fracture surgery I had when I was 12. That healed fine and doesn’t limit me with PT, drill, or carrying a pack. I did note that I still get mild seasonal pollen allergies in late spring (mostly sneezing and itchy eyes). I usually just take over‑the‑counter loratadine as needed and it works well. I also mentioned that I sometimes get motion sickness on long, bumpy rides, so I try to sit near the front of the van and look at the horizon.

For the vitals section, I updated my current measurements from last week’s school wellness screening: height 5’5” and weight 128.0 lbs in regular PT gear, no shoes. Blood pressure was 108/70 and resting pulse was 72. I don’t take any daily prescription meds, just occasional ibuprofen for menstrual cramps.

Let me know if you need me to re‑sign anything or if my mom has to initial the corrections in a specific color pen. I can bring the hard copy to our next Tuesday meeting at Pearson Field.

Thanks for helping make sure everything’s squared away before the activity.

Respectfully,
C/SSgt Emily R. Johnson
CAPID: 653219
Columbia Composite Squadron

metadata.subject: Updated Health Info for Spring Bivouac – Emily Johnson

artifacts.content:
From: Laura Johnson <laura.johnson@example.com>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@example.com>
Date: 2025-03-09 07:18
Subject: Sports physical forms – please fill out tonight

Em,

I printed the sports/activities physical packet that the school nurse emailed about. Since you’re doing JROTC, CAP, and you mentioned maybe joining the spring conditioning group, they need updated height/weight and allergy info.

Please don’t guess on your weight this time like you did in 9th grade. The nurse’s note specifically said they don’t want “about 120-ish” written in. Use the bathroom scale and write down the exact number, even if it’s something random like 123.6 or 131.2. It doesn’t have to be perfect, just not made up. Put it in pounds, not kilograms.

On the allergy section, make sure you explain the pollen thing clearly. Remember last May when your sneezing and watery eyes got so bad during after-school JROTC drill that MSgt. Ramirez had to send you to the office and call me to pick you up? You were miserable. You should write that you’ve got mild seasonal allergies to tree and grass pollen every April–June and that you take 10 mg loratadine from Walgreens most mornings in spring so you don’t have to miss drill or your Riverside Brew & Bagel shifts.

Leave the forms on the kitchen counter before bed so I can sign the parent section. The nurse wants everything turned in by Thursday.

Love,
Mom

metadata.subject: Sports physical forms – please fill out tonight

artifacts.content:
Title: Annual Well-Child Visit & Sports Clearance
Date: 2025-03-14 15:30–16:30
Location: Evergreen Family Health Clinic, Exam Room 4
Attendees: Emily Johnson; Laura Johnson

Description:

Appointment for Emily’s 17-year well-child exam and sports/JROTC clearance.

To bring/do:
- Insurance card and ID
- Immunization record
- List of current meds (loratadine 10 mg most spring mornings for seasonal pollen allergies; occasional ibuprofen for cramps)
- Civil Air Patrol and AFJROTC medical forms

Nurse will:
- Check vision (contacts + backup glasses in backpack)
- Measure height and weight on clinic scale (don’t rely on the home reading of 121.4 lbs from last weekend)
- Take blood pressure, pulse

Dr. Shah will:
- Review last year’s scooter wrist fracture follow-up (no issues)
- Ask about motion sickness on car rides/bumpy flights
- Go over pollen allergy incident from May 2024 when Emily had to leave JROTC drill early because of sneezing, watery eyes, and congestion; confirm loratadine is working so she doesn’t miss school or Riverside Brew & Bagel shifts
- Sign activity clearance form if everything looks good

Note: Clinic sent reminder that they want exact weight in pounds written on the school form, not “around 120.” Emily should step on the scale without shoes/hoodie so the number is accurate for the nurse to record.

metadata.title: Annual Well-Child Visit & Sports Clearance
metadata.location: Evergreen Family Health Clinic, Exam Room 4

artifacts.content:
From: Coach Miller <miller.pe@vansd.org>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@example.com>
Date: 2025-02-20 11:02
Subject: Conditioning club info + health questionnaire

Hi Emily,

Glad to hear you’re interested in joining the spring conditioning group to support your JROTC fitness goals.

Attached is the basic health questionnaire we use. For the weight question, you can use a recent home scale reading; it doesn’t have to match our gym scale exactly. Most students just put whatever they saw within the last week or two (e.g., 119, 124.5, 132, etc.). It’s mainly for tracking progress, not grading.

If you’re uncomfortable sharing the exact number with the group, you can keep it between you, me, and the nurse, but please don’t leave it blank. We’ll do an initial weigh-in and bodyweight strength assessment during the first week anyway.

There’s also a section on allergies and medications. Be sure to mention your seasonal pollen allergy and the loratadine you take in the spring. The nurse mentioned you had a rough time at JROTC drill last May with sneezing and watery eyes and had to go home early, so we want to make sure outdoor workouts around the practice fields don’t trigger that again.

Turn the form in to the PE office by next Thursday, or email a scanned copy if that’s easier.

Best,
Coach Miller

metadata.subject: Conditioning club info + health questionnaire

artifacts.content:
From: MyFitnessPal Notifications <no-reply@myfitnesspal.com>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@example.com>
Date: 2025-01-05 06:41
Subject: Weekly progress report for Emily

Hi Emily,

Here’s your weekly check-in based on the information you logged.

Starting weight (user-entered on 12/01/2024): 135.0 lbs
Current weight (user-entered on 01/04/2025): 131.8 lbs

Great job staying consistent through winter break. Remember that day-to-day fluctuations of a few pounds are normal, especially with changes in water intake and salty café food from your Riverside Brew & Bagel shifts.

You logged 4 days of activity this week:
- 2x JROTC drill practices (estimated 45 minutes each)
- 1x jog around the park near Willow Bend Drive (30 minutes)
- 1x basic strength session (bodyweight squats, push-ups, planks)

You also noted:
- Mild motion sickness during a long car ride – you kept activity light that day.
- Increased sneezing and watery eyes after walking past the practice fields; you added a note that you usually take 10 mg loratadine in spring for your tree and grass pollen allergies so you don’t have to skip JROTC or work.

Reminder: The numbers you enter here are for your personal tracking and may not match the official measurement your doctor or school nurse records for sports or JROTC forms. For medical paperwork, always use the most recent reading from a professional scale.

Keep up the good work,
The MyFitnessPal Team

metadata.subject: Weekly progress report for Emily

artifacts.content:
Title: JROTC Fitness Assessment Day
Date: 2025-04-22 07:00–09:00
Location: Columbia River High School – Main Gym & Track
Attendees: AFJROTC cadets (incl. Emily Johnson)

Description:

Quarterly AFJROTC fitness assessment.

07:00–07:20 Check-in, height/weight station in main gym
- Cadets line up by flight.
- Weights recorded on school scale for internal JROTC tracking only (cadets may see the number but it won’t be announced). Use whatever the scale shows that morning, even if it’s an odd number like 119.7 or 133.3.

07:20–08:30 Fitness events

From: Laura Johnson <laura.johnson@example.com>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@example.com>

Subject: Sports physical forms – please finish tonight

Hey Em,

I printed the sports physical packet your school nurse emailed yesterday and left it on the kitchen table next to Daisy’s leash. Please fill out the student sections before your appointment at Evergreen Family Clinic on Thursday. Dr. Patel needs your info ahead of time, including your height, **current weight**, medications, and any past injuries.

For the weight question, just use what you saw on the scale last weekend when we were at Grandma Patricia’s. I wrote it on the sticky note I stuck to the front of the packet: **“Weight: 121 lb”** so you don’t have to guess. Sign where it says “student signature” and leave the insurance card on top so I remember to grab it. Text me a picture of the completed pages before you head to JROTC drill so I can double‑check everything.

artifacts.content:
Title: Fitness Assessment – JROTC
Date: 2025-04-04 15:30–17:00
Location: Columbia River High School – Main Gym
Attendees: Emily Johnson; AFJROTC Cadet Group; MSgt. Rivera

Notes:

Quarterly PT assessment for all cadets. Wear PT gear (squadron T‑shirt, shorts, running shoes). Bring water bottle and inhalers/meds if needed. Daisy stays home this time!

Planned events:
- Warm‑up and stretching
- 1 mile run
- Push‑ups and sit‑ups (timed)
- Height and **weight check** for fitness tracking

MSgt. Rivera said he’ll record everyone’s height and weight in the squadron spreadsheet so we can see progress over the year. At the last check in January, Emily logged:
- Height: 5’5”
- Weight: **135 lbs** (with shoes on, after lunch)

He reminded everyone not to stress about the number, just to focus on improving run times and strength. Emily wants to beat her mile time from winter (9:12) and is hoping that jogging around the park by Willow Bend Drive has helped.

Reminder to self (Emily):
- Eat something light after last class so I’m not running on an empty stomach.
- Take loratadine in the morning if pollen is bad so allergies don’t mess with breathing during the run.
- Ask MSgt. if motion sickness on buses counts as anything for the health form.

artifacts.content:
From: Evergreen Family Clinic <no-reply@evergreenclinic.org>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@example.com>
Date: 2025-03-14 10:06
Subject: Visit Summary – Sports Physical

Dear Emily Rose Johnson,

Thank you for visiting Evergreen Family Clinic on 03/14/2025 for your school sports physical with Dr. Anika Patel.

Summary of measurements (as recorded today):
- Blood pressure: 110/68
- Pulse: 72 bpm
- Height: 165 cm (5’5”)
- **Weight: 54.4 kg (120 lb)**

You were cleared for non-contact and limited-contact school sports and JROTC physical training, with no restrictions beyond self‑monitoring motion sickness on long car rides. Please continue using over‑the‑counter loratadine as needed for seasonal allergies. Your prior left wrist fracture is fully healed and does not require follow‑up at this time.

General health recommendations:
- Maintain a balanced diet to support your busy schedule (school, barista job at Riverside Brew & Bagel, and Civil Air Patrol activities).
- Aim for at least 8 hours of sleep per night.
- Stay hydrated, particularly during outdoor drill and runs at the park.

If any information above is inaccurate (for example, if you believe your **weight** was recorded incorrectly), please reply to this message or call our office within 7 days so we can update your chart.

This message is for informational purposes only and should not substitute for medical advice from your provider.

Sincerely,
Evergreen Family Clinic

metadata.subject: Visit Summary – Sports Physical

artifacts.content:
Title: Check‑in with Maya – gym & SAT study
Date: 2025-05-02 18:00–19:30
Location: PlanetFit Gym – Mill Plain
Attendees: Emily Johnson; Maya Chen

Notes:

Plan: Meet Maya at the front desk after my shift at Riverside Brew & Bagel. Quick treadmill run, light weights, then camp in the lounge area with SAT prep books.

Maya keeps saying I shouldn’t obsess about the scale, but I still want to track things a little. Last time we went (early April), the gym scale showed:
- Emily: **126 lbs** (in running shoes, evening)
- Maya: 132 lbs

We both laughed because it fluctuates every time, depending on shoes and whether we’ve eaten dinner. I told her I mostly care about feeling stronger for JROTC PT and CAP activities, not the exact **weight** number.

To‑do for Emily:
- Bring small notebook to log workouts and maybe jot down progress (mile time, push‑ups, etc.).
- Don’t forget backup glasses in case contacts get dry after running.
- Text Mom when leaving the gym so she doesn’t worry about me walking home in the dark.

Side note: Try not to overdo it; motion sickness kicked in a bit after that super intense treadmill session last month when I jumped off too fast. Focus on steady pace instead of sprinting.

metadata.title: Check‑in with Maya – gym & SAT study

artifacts.content:
From: emily.johnson17@gmail.com
To: vancouvercadetsafety@cap.gov
Date: 2025-03-09 19:42
Subject: Medical & Membership Info for Spring Bivouac

Hi Capt. Ramirez,

Per your announcement at Tuesday’s meeting, I’m emailing my updated info for the spring bivouac at Camp Rilea.

Full name: Emily Rose Johnson
Squadron: Vancouver Composite Squadron, charter number CA-123

I don’t have any diagnosed food allergies, but I wanted to mention something that happened last year so it’s on your radar. In early March 2024, after Sunday dinner at my grandma Patricia Peters’ house in east Vancouver, I ate a slice of her walnut banana bread. About 20 minutes later my lips got kind of tingly and I noticed a few itchy red spots on my neck. My mom gave me a 10 mg loratadine from her purse and watched me for about an hour. The symptoms went away and we didn’t go to PeaceHealth Southwest Medical Center since it never got worse. Since then I’ve just been avoiding walnuts and I write “no walnuts” on potluck sign-up sheets at school and CAP.

Otherwise, I only have mild seasonal pollen allergies (sneezing/itchy eyes) that I also manage with over-the-counter loratadine. No asthma, no EpiPen, and I’m not on any daily prescription meds.

I’ve attached the medical form you gave us in class and I’ll bring a printed copy, signed by my parents, to the next Tuesday meeting at Pearson Field. Please let me know if you need anything else or if I should list the walnut situation differently on the form.

Thank you,
Emily Johnson
C/Amn, Civil Air Patrol

metadata.subject: Medical & Membership Info for Spring Bivouac

artifacts.content:
From: Capt. Michael Harris <m.harris@wacapwing.org>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson17@example.com>
Date: 2025-01-09 18:42
Subject: Civil Air Patrol – Charter & Unit Info for Application

Hi Emily,

Glad to hear you’re moving forward with your CAP cadet programs and starting to look at scholarship and academy opportunities. As promised after the meeting at Pearson Field, here’s the unit information you’ll need for most online forms:

– Squadron Name: Vancouver Composite Squadron
– Wing: Washington Wing (WAWG)
– Region: Pacific Region
– Unit Charter Number: WA-452
– Unit Meeting Location: Pearson Field Education Center, 201A E Reserve St, Vancouver, WA

Most applications that ask for “charter number” or “unit charter” are referring to the squadron’s identifier above (WA-452), not anything personal to you. Some forms also ask for your CAPID, which is different from the charter number. Your CAPID should be on your membership card and in eServices.

If a form asks for a “school unit number” instead, for JROTC you’ll usually use the AFJROTC unit designation for Columbia River High School, which is WA-20119, not the CAP squadron number. Don’t mix those up; scholarship committees get picky about accurate unit data.

Let me know if you need the Group or Wing mailing address for any of the ROTC or aerospace engineering summer program applications you’re considering. Also, keep in mind that some medical forms will ask about your past injuries—your old wrist fracture should be fine as long as you note that it’s fully healed.

v/r,

Capt. Harris
Vancouver Composite Squadron, WAWG
Civil Air Patrol, USAF Auxiliary

metadata.subject: Civil Air Patrol – Charter & Unit Info for Application

metadata.title: JROTC / CAP Paperwork Night – Unit & Charter Numbers

artifacts.content:
From: scholarships@capfoundation.org
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson17@example.com>
Date: 2025-02-11 09:27
Subject: Incomplete Application – Charter/Unit Number Clarification

Dear Cadet Johnson,

Thank you for submitting your initial application for the 2025 Civil Air Patrol STEM Career Exploration Scholarship. Before your application can move forward to the selection committee, we need clarification on the “Charter/Unit Number” field.

On your form, you entered: “WA-20119.” This appears to be the AFJROTC unit designation for Columbia River High School, not your CAP squadron’s charter number. For our records, we require the CAP unit charter number associated with your home squadron.

For example, a typical CAP charter number looks like “WA-452” or “OR-073,” and is associated with your local composite, cadet, or senior squadron. Please log back into the scholarship portal, navigate to the “Member Information” section, and update the charter number field with your CAP squadron’s charter.

If you are unsure of this information, you may find it on your squadron’s website, by asking your squadron commander, or by checking your unit’s listing on the Civil Air Patrol national site. Do not enter your personal CAPID or your JROTC school unit number in this field.

Once you have corrected the charter number, your application status will change from “Incomplete – Pending Member Info” to “Submitted.” If it does not update within 24 hours, please reply to this email.

Sincerely,

CAP Foundation Scholarship Team

metadata.subject: Incomplete Application – Charter/Unit Number Clarification

artifacts.content:
From: 1st Lt. Karen Morales <k.morales@wacapwing.org>
To: Vancouver Composite Cadets <vancadets@wacapwing.org>
Date: 2024-12-02 21:03
Subject: Admin Night – Verify Your CAPID & Charter Info

Cadets,

Next Tuesday’s meeting (10 Dec) will be an Admin Night focused on making sure everyone’s records are squared away before we close out the year. Please bring a photo ID (school ID is fine) and your CAP membership card if you have a hard copy.

We’ll be verifying three things:
1. Your CAPID (personal member number)
2. Our squadron charter number for forms
3. Your contact information (email, phone, and mailing address)

For reference during the meeting, our unit information is:
- Squadron Name: Vancouver Composite Squadron
- Charter Number: WA-452
- Wing: Washington Wing (WAWG)

Some of you, including Emily, have mixed up the squadron charter with the AFJROTC unit number at your high school (WA-20119). Remember: the charter number above is what you use on CAP-specific forms, encampment applications, and most aviation scholarship paperwork.

We’ll also briefly go over how to handle questions about medical history on activity applications. Old injuries like a fully healed wrist fracture usually aren’t disqualifying, but they must be reported accurately.

Uniform: ABUs or blues, whichever you currently have that meets regs.

v/r,

1st Lt. Morales
Administration Officer
Vancouver Composite Squadron, WAWG

metadata.subject: Admin Night – Verify Your CAPID & Charter Info

artifacts.content:
From: Capt. Melissa Harding <melissa.harding@wawgcap.org>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.johnson17@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-09-03 18:42
Subject: Squadron Admin Night – Membership Docs Needed

Hi Emily,

Good work at PT last night. Before we finalize your transfer from the introductory cadet status to full active status, I need you to double‑check a couple of admin items for our records.

For next Tuesday’s meeting, please bring:

1. A printed copy of your CAP membership card (the one that shows your CAPID and your **unit charter number**). If you only have the temporary card, that’s fine as long as the unit is listed as **WA-461**.
2. Any updated contact info for your parents, especially your mom’s cell number, so we can reach her if you have issues during activities or if your allergies flare up at field exercises.
3. A copy of the permission form for overnight activities. We still have the one you used for the leadership weekend in August, but it’s tied to the old orientation unit **OR-219**, so we need the new one.

Please don’t guess on the charter number. Use exactly what’s on the membership card or the squadron website under “Unit Information.” If you’re unsure, you can also log into eServices and look at the “My Units” page – it should list **WA-461** as your current squadron.

See you next week,

Capt. Harding
Vancouver Composite Squadron, Civil Air Patrol

metadata.subject: Squadron Admin Night – Membership Docs Needed

artifacts.content:
From: National CAP Membership <membership@capnhq.gov>
To: Emily R. Johnson <emily.johnson17@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-11-21 09:16
Subject: Welcome to Civil Air Patrol – Provisional Cadet Assignment

Dear Emily Johnson,

Welcome to Civil Air Patrol and thank you for joining! This message confirms receipt of your online cadet application and dues payment. Your CAPID and permanent membership card will be mailed once your unit completes the final approval.

For the next 60 days, you are listed as a **provisional cadet** attached to the recruiting unit:

Unit Name: Portland Metro Flight
Charter Number: OR-312
Wing: Oregon Wing

This provisional charter number (OR-312) may appear on temporary documentation and in your online account until your transfer to your home squadron in Washington Wing is processed. Your local squadron commander will submit the transfer request after you attend three meetings and complete your introductory safety briefing.

Please note: Any orientation flights or training activities you participate in during this provisional period will be recorded under charter OR-312. Once your home unit assignment is complete, future activities will be credited to that unit instead. This has no effect on your time‑in‑grade, rank eligibility, or long‑term record.

If you notice any discrepancies in your personal information (spelling of your name, address on Willow Bend Drive, or emergency contact details for Mark or Laura Johnson), reply to this email within 10 business days so we can correct them before your plastic card is printed.

Sincerely,

National Headquarters
Civil Air Patrol – United States Air Force Auxiliary

metadata.subject: Welcome to Civil Air Patrol – Provisional Cadet Assignment

artifacts.content:
Title: CAP eServices Setup & Charter Lookup
Date: 2025-01-07 19:00-20:00
Location: Emily’s bedroom desk / laptop
Attendees: Emily Johnson

Notes:

Log into CAP eServices for the first time and finish setting up the account before next week’s squadron meeting.

To‑do list during this hour:

- Find the email from National HQ with temporary login and verify my CAPID.
- On the “My Units” page, confirm that the provisional assignment still shows **Charter OR-312** and not the Vancouver squadron yet.
- Write down the charter number OR-312 and CAPID on a sticky note to bring to the next meeting in case Capt. Harding asks.
- Update personal info: address on Willow Bend Drive, cell number, emergency contacts (Mark and Laura), allergy info (pollen, motion sickness on longer van rides), and the old wrist surgery from when I broke my arm at 12.
- Watch the short tutorial video on how to request transcripts of achievements so I can track promotions.

Reminder: Once the transfer to the Washington Wing unit goes through, the charter number here will change. Don’t use OR-312 on any permanent forms for school JROTC credit; that paperwork needs whatever charter the Vancouver squadron uses. Ask at the next meeting which charter number to put down on the school activity form.

From: Maj. Robert Keller <robert.keller@wawgcap.org>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.johnson17@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-02-14 15:09
Subject: Verification Needed – Charter Number on School Form

Hi Emily,

Your JROTC instructor faxed over the community service verification form so we can sign off your Civil Air Patrol hours for school credit. Thanks for being on top of that.

I noticed that on the line labeled “CAP Unit Charter #,” someone wrote **WA-219**. That’s actually the charter for the Longview squadron, not ours. Our Vancouver Composite Squadron charter is **WA-461**, and that’s what should be used for any school or scholarship paperwork.

Before I sign and return the form, can you please confirm whether your school counselor needs the corrected charter number typed in or if it’s okay for me to neatly cross out WA-219 and write WA-461 next to my signature? I don’t want this to cause any delay for your JROTC credit.

Also, remind your counselor that your CAP activities are separate from your part‑time job at Riverside Brew & Bagel; the hours for the café shouldn’t be mixed with your service hours for our unit.

Once you reply, I’ll finish the form and email a scanned copy back to the counseling office.

v/r,

Maj. Robert Keller
Deputy Commander for Cadets
Vancouver Composite Squadron
Charter: WA-461

Hi Cadet Johnson,

Cadet Johnson,

Until National Headquarters finishes processing your membership, you may see different temporary numbers on sign-in sheets or Google Docs. Description:

To-do block to finish Civil Air Patrol paperwork before the end-of-month deadline.

Checklist:
- Log into eServices and verify my CAP membership status.
- Find the email from National HQ with the digital membership card; the CAPID on that card is the one I need to use from now on.
- Make sure I’m not accidentally reusing the fake CAPID 135792468 that Sgt. Miller put on the whiteboard during the safety briefing as an example.
- Update "Member Information > CAPID" field on CAPF 160 and CAPF 161. The form currently has my student ID written there, which is wrong.

From: mom.johnson.family@gmail.com
To: emily.r.johnson08@gmail.com
Date: 2025-02-24 07:18
Subject: Printed your CAP medical packet

Em,

I printed the CAP medical packet you left open on the dining room laptop. A couple of things I noticed while it was coming out of the printer:

1. On the first page where it says "Member Information > CAPID," you wrote down 999888777. I’m pretty sure that’s the number you made up when you were practicing filling out the form last week, not the real one from your membership card.
2. On one of the later pages, you scribbled your school ID in the CAPID box and then crossed it out. Might want to reprint that page so it’s not confusing for whoever reviews it.

You might want to bring your actual CAP card home from your backpack so we can copy the correct ID number neatly in blue ink. I don’t want them to think you’re being careless before that big field training weekend.

Also, I saw where you described your allergies. It looks good that you included the bit about getting mild hives and itching on your hands when you use the new lemon-scented sanitizer at Riverside Brew & Bagel and how it cleared up after you rinsed and took a loratadine. That sounds thorough but not scary.

I left the printed packet on your pillow so you don’t forget it tonight.

Love,
Mom

artifacts.content:
From: 123recruiting@cawgcap.org
To: emily.r.johnson08@gmail.com
Subject: Civil Air Patrol Cadet Membership Pre-Approval
Date: Mon, 26 Aug 2024 18:42:11 -0700

Dear Emily Johnson,

Thank you for starting your online cadet membership application with Civil Air Patrol. Our records show your preliminary Washington Wing applicant ID as **WA-APP-87265431**. Please note this is NOT your permanent CAPID; a separate, shorter numeric CAPID will be assigned only after your unit commander completes the final approval step.

Next steps:
1. Print and sign the Cadet Membership Application form.
2. Have a parent or legal guardian sign where indicated.
3. Bring the completed form and a copy of your school ID to your local squadron meeting.

Squadron information on file:
- Unit: Pearson Field Composite Squadron
- Charter: WAWA-094
- Point of Contact: Capt. Michael Alvarez

Once your paperwork is verified, National Headquarters will issue your official membership card and CAPID. Until then, please use the applicant ID WA-APP-87265431 for any questions about your application status. Do not enter this number into eServices where a CAPID is requested; that field must remain blank until your permanent ID is generated.

If you experience motion sickness during flights or activities, please notify your squadron staff so appropriate accommodations can be made. Many cadets successfully participate with minor medical considerations like allergies or past orthopedic injuries.

We are excited to see your interest in aerospace education and leadership.

Respectfully,

Civil Air Patrol Recruiting Team
National Headquarters

metadata.subject: Civil Air Patrol Cadet Membership Pre-Approval

artifacts.content:
From: malvarez@wawa094.cap.gov
To: emily.johnson@vansd.org
Subject: eServices Login & Temporary Cadet ID
Date: Thu, 12 Sep 2024 21:07:03 -0700

Hi Emily,

Great job at your second Pearson Field Composite Squadron meeting last night. As promised, here is the information you’ll need to get started in eServices.

Your **temporary cadet reference number** for our squadron records is: PFCS-17-0094. This is an internal tracking code I use for new cadets and is NOT your official Civil Air Patrol CAPID. National Headquarters will assign your permanent CAPID after your background/verification steps clear and your dues payment fully processes.

For now, follow these steps:
1. Go to https://www.capnhq.gov and click “First Time Users.”
2. When prompted for CAPID, select the option indicating you have not yet received one.
3. Use your school email (this one) as your primary contact.

Once your actual CAPID is issued, it will be a purely numeric sequence, different from PFCS-17-0094. We’ll update your membership form and your JROTC cross-enrollment record at Columbia River High School at that time.

Also, remember to bring your backup glasses to next week’s safety briefing; we’ll be doing a night-vision demonstration, and contacts sometimes get dry.

Let me know if you have trouble logging in.

v/r,

Capt Michael Alvarez, CAP
Deputy Commander for Cadets
Pearson Field Composite Squadron (WAWA-094)

artifacts.content:
Title: CAP New Cadet Orientation & ID Check
Date: 2024-10-02 18:30-20:30
Location: Pearson Field Composite Squadron, Classroom B, Vancouver, WA
Attendees: Emily Rose Johnson; Capt. Michael Alvarez; New Cadet Group

Notes:

Agenda:
- 18:30–18:45: Sign-in and temporary roster numbers
- 18:45–19:15: Safety briefing and uniform overview
- 19:15–20:00: eServices overview and CAPID explanation
- 20:00–20:30: Q&A with senior members

Instructions to Emily:

1. Bring your government-issued photo ID (school ID is fine) and the email from National HQ that lists your **application tracking number**. The number we have on the preliminary roster for you right now is **TRK-208-6715**. This is used for tonight’s headcount only and is not the same as your official Civil Air Patrol CAPID.

2. Do NOT write TRK-208-6715 in any box labeled “CAPID” on forms. That space must stay blank until National assigns your permanent member number. We’ll help you update your records once your actual CAPID appears in the system.

metadata.title: CAP New Cadet Orientation & ID Check
metadata.location: Pearson Field Composite Squadron, Classroom B, Vancouver, WA

artifacts.content:
From: membership@capnhq.gov
To: emily.r.johnson08@gmail.com
Subject: Civil Air Patrol Cadet Membership – Processing Update
Date: Fri, 18 Oct 2024 09:24:55 -0500

Dear Emily R. Johnson,

Thank you for your recent Civil Air Patrol cadet membership application. This message confirms that your payment has been received and your file is moving through the final verification stage.

For reference, your **National Case Number** is: NCN-7742035. This is used by our Membership Services team to track your application and is different from your CAPID. Your permanent CAPID (a separate, purely numeric identifier) will be issued in a subsequent email along with instructions for printing your membership card.

Important:
- Do not list NCN-7742035 in any fields labeled “CAPID” on squadron forms, online profiles, or school JROTC cross-enrollment paperwork.
- Your squadron commander will be automatically notified once your CAPID is generated.

If any personal information (address, school, or emergency contact) changes before you receive your official member number, reply to this email with the updated details so we can correct your record before the CAPID is assigned.

We understand many new cadets are balancing school, work, and CAP activities. Please do not schedule orientation flights or certain training events until your CAPID is active, as these activities must be logged under the correct member number.

We look forward to welcoming you as an active cadet member.

Sincerely,

Membership Services
Civil Air Patrol National Headquarters

metadata.subject: Civil Air Patrol Cadet Membership – Processing Update

artifacts.content:
From: Laura Johnson <laura.johnson@email.com>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@schoolmail.org>
Date: 2025-02-11 07:18
Subject: Eye doctor appointment after school

Hey Em,

I just got off the phone with Vancouver Vision Clinic and confirmed your eye exam for next Wednesday at 3:45 p.m. They said it’s been almost two years since your last checkup, which explains why you’ve been squinting at the TV again.

The receptionist asked me to confirm your info, including your eye color, and I told her you have **green** eyes like Grandpa Daniel did. She laughed and said they see a lot of brown and blue, but green is still kind of special. Anyway, they’ll have you read the chart and check if your prescription needs to be adjusted for your contacts.

Make sure you bring your backup **brown-framed** glasses in your backpack in case they want you to leave your contacts out for a bit. Also, don’t forget to mention the headaches you sometimes get after long computer science homework sessions. It might just be screen time, but let them rule out anything else.

I’ll pick you up right after school and we’ll head straight there, so maybe grab a snack at lunch to hold you over. Text me if JROTC has anything that might conflict and we’ll reschedule.

Love you,
Mom

metadata.subject: Eye doctor appointment after school

artifacts.content:
From: Maya Patel <maya.patel17@chatmail.com>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@schoolmail.org>
Date: 2024-10-28 21:06
Subject: Yearbook superlatives + random question

Em,

I’m filling out the online form for yearbook superlatives and it reminded me of that filter you used on Snapchat the other day that made your eyes look super bright **blue**. Honestly, it weirded me out because I’m so used to your normal **greenish** eyes glaring at me over chemistry notes when I mess up a problem.

The form has this silly “describe your best friend” box and I almost wrote: “Short, aviation-obsessed, permanently in a messy bun, and the girl with the intense **green** eyes who can stare down a JROTC flight.” I’m not actually putting that (unless you dare me), but it made me laugh.

Random: do you remember what color you put down for eye color on your CAP application? I’m filling out my driver’s permit stuff and the options are just brown, blue, green, gray, and other. Mine are boring brown, but yours always look more **green** in the sun, especially at Pearson Field. I swear they matched that green flight jacket you tried on.

Anyway, text me if you want to coordinate outfits for picture retakes. I’m avoiding anything that clashes with your **green** eyes this time, because last year’s neon hoodie situation was tragic.

– Maya

metadata.subject: Yearbook superlatives + random question

artifacts.content:
Title: DMV ID Card & Eye Color Form
Date: 2025-03-07 15:30–16:30
Location: Washington State Department of Licensing, Vancouver
Attendees: Emily Johnson, Mark Johnson

Notes:

Appointment to get Emily’s first official state ID card before she starts doing more solo driving practice and CAP activities that require photo ID.

Checklist from Dad:
- Bring birth certificate
- Social Security card
- School ID
- Proof of address (utility bill)
- Decide what to put for eye color on the form

Emily and Dad talked last week about the eye color section because the form only has basic options (brown, blue, green, gray, other). Dad said that in regular light, her eyes look clearly **green**, not brown, and that’s what he wants her to pick so it matches what an officer would see if they ever checked her ID. Emily joked that in some selfies they look almost gray, but she agreed **green** is the best match.

Reminder: Emily should remove her contacts if they’re bothering her after school; she can wear her glasses in the photo. Mark noted in the family group chat that her **green** eyes will probably stand out more if she avoids red, orange, or bright pink shirts in the picture.

metadata.title: DMV ID Card & Eye Color Form
metadata.location: Washington State Department of Licensing, Vancouver

artifacts.content:
From: 927th Composite Squadron Admin <admin@927capsq.org>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@schoolmail.org>
Date: 2025-01-05 16:12
Subject: Incomplete information on CAP Emergency Services Form

Cadet Johnson,

During our review of your updated Civil Air Patrol Emergency Services qualification packet, we noticed a few missing or inconsistent fields that must be corrected before your next training exercise.

Specifically:
1. Eye color: You left this blank on the new form, but your previous membership record lists your eyes as **green**. Please confirm that this is still accurate. If so, write "GREEN" in block letters in the eye color field.
2. Hair color: Currently listed as "light brown," which matches the description provided by your JROTC instructor, so no change needed.
3. Corrective lenses: You indicated that you wear contact lenses. Please ensure you also check the box for "glasses" since you stated in your medical history that you carry **black-framed** backup glasses in your backpack.

Accurate eye color (in your case, **green**) and corrective lens information are critical in case of an emergency search and rescue situation. This data is used for identification purposes if you are ever separated from your team.

Bring the corrected form to the next Tuesday meeting at Pearson Field, or scan and email it back to this address before then.

Respectfully,

2d Lt. Karen Morales, CAP
Squadron Administration Officer
927th Composite Squadron

metadata.subject: Incomplete information on CAP Emergency Services Form

artifacts.content:
Title: Photography Club Portrait Session – Eye Color Reference
Date: 2024-11-09 14:00–16:00
Location: Columbia River High School – Art Room 214
Attendees: Emily Johnson, Photography Club members

Notes:

Emily volunteered to be one of the portrait subjects for Maya’s photography project on “Faces of CRHS.” Maya is experimenting with lighting setups to see how different eye colors show up on camera.

Planned shots for Emily:
- Close-up portraits in natural window light to highlight her **green** eyes
- Side profile with softbox to compare how the **green** tones look against a darker background
- A few test shots where Emily wears her **blue-tinted** safety glasses from chemistry just for fun, to see how they change the appearance of her eye color

Maya wrote in her project notebook that Emily’s eyes are “a clear **green**, not brown, and they catch the light easily,” which makes them ideal for demonstrating how camera sensors handle subtle color variations.

Reminder for Emily: Avoid heavy eye makeup that might distract from the natural **green** color; just basic mascara is fine. Bring contact lens solution and your glasses in case your contacts dry out under the studio lights.

Optional: If time allows, Maya wants to do a comparison shot with another student who has bright **blue** eyes to show the difference side by side.

metadata.title: Photography Club Portrait Session – Eye Color Reference
metadata.location: Columbia River High School – Art Room 214

artifacts.content:
From: Laura Johnson <laura.johnson@example.com>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@example.com>
Date: 2025-03-02 19:18
Subject: DMV form – double‑check everything tonight

Hey Em,

I printed the Washington DMV “Intermediate Driver License” application and left it on the kitchen table by Daisy’s leash. Please fill out your portion tonight so we can swing by the licensing office after school on Thursday.

They want a bunch of “basic member information” on page 2 – height, **eye color**, hair color, etc. For eye color I wrote in **GREEN** when I did the online pre‑application because that’s what it looks like on your school photo and what the nurse put on your immunization card years ago. If you think it should say something else, we can ask them at the counter, but don’t overthink it. They just don’t want it left blank.

Also, make sure you copy your full legal name exactly like it appears on your Social Security card (middle name spelled out, not just “R.”), and use our home address on Willow Bend. Bring your glasses or contacts case in case they do a vision screening.

I know paperwork is annoying, but this is the last step before you can drive yourself to JROTC and work instead of me playing chauffeur. :)

Love you,
Mom

P.S. I stuck your old hospital discharge summary from when you broke your wrist in the folder in case they want extra ID, but I doubt they will.

metadata.subject: DMV form – double‑check everything tonight

artifacts.content:
From: Riverside Brew & Bagel HR <hr@riversidebrewbagel.com>
To: Emily Johnson <emily.r.johnson@example.com>
Date: 2024-11-08 10:41
Subject: New Hire Packet – Please Confirm Details

Hi Emily,

Welcome officially to Riverside Brew & Bagel! Attached is your new hire packet. Before payroll can finalize your file, we need you to confirm some basic information you wrote on your initial application.

Here’s what we currently have:

Name: Emily Rose Johnson  
Position: Part‑time Barista  
Start Date: 11/15/2024  
Hair Color: Light Brown  
Eye Color: **BLUE**  
Height: 5’5”  
Primary Emergency Contact: Laura Johnson (mother)

Please reply to this email by Sunday confirming that the above is accurate, or correcting anything that’s wrong. If we don’t hear from you, we’ll assume it’s correct and submit it to our payroll provider as‑is.

Note: The eye color and hair color fields are only used for internal ID badge printing and to help managers identify staff in case of emergency. They do not affect scheduling or your eligibility to work. If your appearance changes significantly (for example, you dye your hair a very different color or start wearing glasses instead of contacts), just let us know so we can update your file photo.

We’re excited to have you on the team and look forward to seeing you at orientation next Thursday at 4:00 p.m.

Best regards,

Kara Mitchell  
HR & Scheduling Coordinator  
Riverside Brew & Bagel

metadata.subject: New Hire Packet – Please Confirm Details

artifacts.content:
Title: School Picture Retake Day – Verify Info Card
Date: 2024-10-03 07:45-09:00
Location: Columbia River High School – Auxiliary Gym
Attendees: Emily Johnson

Notes:

Emily – remember to actually bring the little Lifetouch info card this time. Last month the photographer just guessed on your details when you forgot to fill it out, which is why your proof sheet says your eyes are **BROWN** and lists your height as 5’3”. We should get it right on the retake so your student ID matches what’s on your other documents.

To‑do before homeroom:
- Fill out the printed card in blue or black ink.  
- Double‑check spelling of your full name and grade (11).  
- For eye color, mark the correct bubble instead of letting them pick for you. Last time the assistant said almost everyone just goes with brown, which is lazy.  
- Wear your contacts and maybe bring your backup glasses in your backpack, just in case your eyes get dry.

Mom already paid online for the retake package, so you don’t need to bring money. Give the photographer the confirmation number from the email on your phone if they ask.

Don’t forget JROTC drill right after second period; you won’t have time to redo your hair, so plan the bun accordingly.

metadata.title: School Picture Retake Day – Verify Info Card
metadata.location: Columbia River High School – Auxiliary Gym

Cadet Johnson,

Current data on file:

Name: Johnson, Emily R.  
Grade: C/Amn

Hair: Lt. Brown  
Eyes: **GRAY**  
Height: 65 in.  
Weight: [not displayed]

If any of the above information is inaccurate, reply to this email by 18 January with the corrections. For eye color, please choose from the standard options: Blue, Brown, Gray, Green, or Other. We understand that some cadets have mixed or hard‑to‑classify eye colors; in those cases, select the closest match.

Accurate eye and hair color are helpful during activities that require visual identification (field exercises, encampment accountability, etc.). This information is not used for promotions or assignment decisions.

If you have recently updated your legal name or address with the school district or DMV, please notify us so we can keep your CAP record consistent. Also, if you experience any motion sickness during upcoming orientation flights, let your flight commander know in advance so we can seat you appropriately.

1st Lt. Karen Liu, CAP

artifacts.content:
Title: Optometrist Appointment – Update Contacts Prescription
Date: 2025-02-19 15:30-16:15
Location: Vancouver Vision Clinic – Mill Plain Blvd
Attendees: Emily Johnson, Laura Johnson

Notes:

Annual eye exam for Emily. She’s been squinting at the board in chemistry again and complaining about headaches on days she forgets her contacts. Remember to bring:

- Current glasses (black frames)  
- Contact lens boxes with prescriptions  
- Insurance card  
- List of over‑the‑counter meds she takes for allergies

Ask Dr. Patel to double‑check how her **BLUE‑GREEN** eye color is listed in their system; last year the tech just clicked “blue” in the dropdown. It would be good if their chart matches what ends up on her driver’s license and school ID, even if different places round it differently.

Also mention:
- Occasional motion sickness during long car rides and bumpy flights.  
- History of left wrist surgery at age 12 (probably irrelevant to vision but stays on her chart).  
- That she sometimes gets dry, itchy eyes in spring from pollen.

Mom: if they dilate her pupils, she may not be able to drive afterward even once she gets her license, so plan to stay and drive home. Emily has work at Riverside Brew & Bagel at 5:30 p.m., so we’ll need to be out of there on time.

Confirm next year’s appointment before leaving.

metadata.title: Optometrist Appointment – Update Contacts Prescription
metadata.location: Vancouver Vision Clinic – Mill Plain Blvd
